title: Afeyan, Nagler & Tu (2025): Spatial RCC analysis: Ligand and Receptor Colocalization
text:
Introduction
This is a book that contains all code to reproduce figures related to spatial analysis of TCR data associated with Afeyan, Nagler & Tu, et al (2025)
Note that while the code is identical, this book was rendered after final manuscript figures were generated and thus minor differences may arise, particularly for steps that are non-deterministic or rely on random number generators.

title: Afeyan, Nagler & Tu (2025): Spatial RCC analysis: Ligand and Receptor Colocalization
section: Tertiary lymphoid structures are reservoirs of stem-like tumor-specific T cells in renal cell carcinoma
text:
Tertiary lymphoid structures are reservoirs of stem-like tumor-specific T cells in renal cell carcinoma
Alexander B. Afeyan†, Adi Nagler†, Chloe R. Tu†, Gabriel Roberti De Oliveira, Berkay Simsek, Maxwell D. Seager, Nourhan El Ahmar, Haley E. Sax, Emma Lin, Amit Sud, Mehdi Borji, Cleo Forman, Sophia Liu, Patrick A. Ott, Toni K. Choueiri, Jennifer G. Abelin, Richard Burack, Shuqiang Li, Kenneth J. Livak, Svitlana Tyekucheva, Derin B. Keskin, Fei Chen, Michael B. Atkins, Jeremy M. Simon, Sabina Signoretti, Giacomo Oliveira°, David A. Braun‡°, Catherine J. Wu‡°

Abstract
Tertiary lymphoid structures (TLS) are associated with improved response to immune checkpoint blockade across solid tumors1–3, but how they impact the phenotypic properties of tumor-specific T cells remains unknown. Across 24 treatment-naïve renal cell carcinoma (RCC) tumors, TLS-containing tumors were more heavily infiltrated by exhausted CD8+ T cells with a reduced terminal exhaustion transcriptional program compared to TLS- tumors. Specificity screening of 554 T cell clonotypes expanded within the microenvironment of 6 RCC tumors revealed 82 TCRs reactive against tumor cells and/or RCC antigens. A subset of tumor-specific T cell clonotypes (12%) was enriched within TLS, and these expressed an increased program of stem-like progenitor exhaustion, associated with favorable anti-tumor immunity. However, in 60 independent RCC tumors, macrophages within tumor margins of TLS-containing tumors bore an inferred immunosuppressive phenotype, and were colocalized with exhausted putative tumor-reactive T cells in a subgroup that was further analyzed, thus supporting this mode of immune evasion as a counterbalance to T cell immune pressure. Our data reveal TLS as reservoirs of tumor-specific T cells with stem-like progenitor features that could be leveraged by T cell immunotherapies.

section: 4.1 sceasy’s convertFormat is currently incompatible with Seurat V5 as of March 3rd 2026 as the ‘slot’ argument of ‘GetAssayData()’ was deprecated. An alternative is to use the package anndataR instead. It requires R v4.5, so I won’t be running it. I’ll be using older objects I had previously converted to h5ad. The RNA assay should be identical across my older and newer objects.

title: 2  Calculate ligand and receptor spatial colocalization
section: 7.1 This particular list is a subset of interactions from LIANA, selected by their source and target cell types and aggregate rank.
text:
7.1 This particular list is a subset of interactions from LIANA, selected by their source and target cell types and aggregate rank.

LRs=pd.read_csv('../objects/LRs_to_investigate.csv',index_col=0)
LRs['pair']=LRs['ligand.complex']+'--'+LRs['receptor.complex']
pairs=pd.DataFrame(LRs['pair'].unique())
pairs.columns=['LR']
pairs['ligand']=pairs['LR'].apply(lambda x: x.split('--')[0])
pairs['receptor']=pairs['LR'].apply(lambda x: x.split('--')[1])
pairs.set_index('LR',inplace=True)

library(tibble)



5 Import data from python code

# ChatGPT created this
# Define folder path
folder_path <- "../objects/LR_output_10_beads"

# Get all CSV files from the folder
# Chloe's update: ".csv" --> "results.*csv"
csv_files <- list.files(folder_path, pattern = "results.*csv", full.names = TRUE)

# Initialize list to store extracted data
result_list <- list()

# Function to extract core name pattern
extract_core_name <- function(name) {
  matches <- regmatches(name, regexec("\\d+_(Puck\\d+_\\d+[A-Z]{2})_.*", name))
  if (length(matches[[1]]) > 1) {
    return(matches[[1]][2])  # Extracts "Puck10_102TA", "Puck5_90MB", etc.
  }
  return(NA)
}

# Loop through each CSV file
for (file in csv_files) {
  # Read CSV file
  data <- read.csv(file, stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
  
  # Extract the file name (without full path) for naming
  file_name <- data$LR[1]
  
  # Ensure the data has a column containing the relevant name
  if (!"puck" %in% colnames(data)) {
    stop("Ensure the CSV files contain the relevant column name!")
  }
  
  # Extract core names
  data$core_name <- sapply(data$puck, extract_core_name)

  # Filter out NA values (invalid rows)
  data <- data[!is.na(data$core_name), ]
  
  # Add a new column indicating the source file (to track where data came from)
  data$source_file <- file_name
  
  # Group by core name and split into sub-dataframes (4-row groups)
  grouped_data <- split(data, data$core_name)

  # Merge with existing list entries if they exist
  for (core_name in names(grouped_data)) {
    if (!is.null(result_list[[core_name]])) {
      # Append the named dataframe to the existing core name list
      result_list[[core_name]][[file_name]] <- grouped_data[[core_name]]
    } else {
      # Create a new list for the core name with a named dataframe
      result_list[[core_name]] <- list()
      result_list[[core_name]][[file_name]] <- grouped_data[[core_name]]
    }
  }
}



6 Format

new_result_list <- list()

# Function to extract and shorten the core name (removing final A/B letter)
extract_short_name <- function(core_name) {
  matches <- regmatches(core_name, regexec("Puck\\d+_(\\d+[TM])", core_name))
  if (length(matches[[1]]) > 1) {
    return(matches[[1]][2])  # Extracts "108T", "102T", etc.
  }
  return(NA)
}

for (core_name in names(result_list)) {
  short_name <- extract_short_name(core_name)  # Extract patient group name
  
  if (is.na(short_name)) next  # Skip if extraction fails

  # Initialize list to store dataframes for this patient group
  patient_dfs <- list()
  
  # Iterate over each named dataframe (corresponding to a CSV file)
  for (file_name in names(result_list[[core_name]])) {
    df <- result_list[[core_name]][[file_name]]

    # Ensure required columns exist
    if (!all(c("pval", "distance", "LR") %in% colnames(df))) {
      next  # Skip if the dataframe is missing necessary columns
    }
    
    # Create column names dynamically based on core_name and distance
    df <- df %>%
      mutate(new_column_name = paste0(core_name, "_dis", distance)) %>%
      select(LR, new_column_name, pval) %>%
      pivot_wider(names_from = new_column_name, values_from = pval)

    # Append to patient_dfs list
    patient_dfs <- append(patient_dfs, list(df))
  }
  
  # If we collected data, merge all patient dataframes **properly**
  if (length(patient_dfs) > 0) {
    patient_df <- bind_rows(patient_dfs)  # Correctly stack all dataframes

    # Ensure no duplicate rows by keeping first non-NA value per column
    patient_df <- patient_df %>%
      group_by(LR) %>%
      summarise(across(everything(), ~ dplyr::first(na.omit(.), default = NA)), .groups = "drop")

    # Store the final combined dataframe in new_result_list
    new_result_list[[core_name]] <- patient_df
  }
}

perpatient.list <- list()
P102T <- new_result_list[grep("102T", names(new_result_list))]
P107T <- new_result_list[grep("107T", names(new_result_list))]
P108T <- new_result_list[grep("108T", names(new_result_list))]
P109T <- new_result_list[grep("109T", names(new_result_list))]
P110T <- new_result_list[grep("110T", names(new_result_list))]
P111T <- new_result_list[grep("111T", names(new_result_list))]

perpatient.list[["102T"]] <- purrr::reduce(P102T, full_join, by = "LR")
perpatient.list[["107T"]] <- purrr::reduce(P107T, full_join, by = "LR")
perpatient.list[["108T"]] <- purrr::reduce(P108T, full_join, by = "LR")
perpatient.list[["109T"]] <- purrr::reduce(P109T, full_join, by = "LR")
perpatient.list[["110T"]] <- purrr::reduce(P110T, full_join, by = "LR")
perpatient.list[["111T"]] <- purrr::reduce(P111T, full_join, by = "LR")



7 Browns method

library(ActivePathways)

patients <- c("102T", "107T", "108T", "109T", "110T", "111T")
distances <- c("dis15", "dis30", "dis100", "dis300")

# Browns method of combining dependent p-vals
pval_list <- list()
for(pt in patients){
  dist_list <- list()
  for(dist in distances){
    pval_mat <- perpatient.list[[pt]] %>%
      select(LR, ends_with(dist)) %>%
      column_to_rownames("LR") %>%
      replace(is.na(.), 1) %>%
      as.matrix()
  
    adj_pval <- merge_p_values(scores = pval_mat, method = "Brown")
    
    dist_list <- append(dist_list, list(adj_pval))
  }
  names(dist_list) <- distances
  pval_list <- append(pval_list, list(dist_list))
}

names(pval_list) <- patients



8 FDR correction

# Perform a Benjamini-Hochberg correction to understand which meet significance in the context of a dataset
padj_bh <- function(df){
  corrected <- p.adjust(df, method = "BH", n = length(df)) %>%
    as.data.frame() %>%
    rownames_to_column("LR") %>%
    dplyr::rename("BH" = ".") 
  return(corrected)
  }

corrected_list <- lapply(pval_list, function(inner) {
  lapply(inner, padj_bh)
})



9 Merge lists per patient

corrected_df_list <- list()
for(pt in patients){
  df <- corrected_list[[pt]][["dis15"]] %>%
    dplyr::rename("BH_dis15" = "BH") %>%
    full_join(corrected_list[[pt]][["dis30"]], by = "LR") %>%
    dplyr::rename("BH_dis30" = "BH") %>%
    full_join(corrected_list[[pt]][["dis100"]], by = "LR") %>%
    dplyr::rename("BH_dis100" = "BH") %>%
    full_join(corrected_list[[pt]][["dis300"]], by = "LR") %>%
    dplyr::rename("BH_dis300" = "BH") 
  
  corrected_df_list <- append(corrected_df_list, list(df))
}
names(corrected_df_list) <- patients



10 Filter for the most important interactions to plot

interactions.list <- openxlsx::read.xlsx("/n/data2/dfci/medonc/cwu/chloe/slideseq_data/tcr_analysis/RCC_neovax_pucks/analysis_110624/scripts/05_LR_analysis/LIANAinteractions.TLSvnonTLS.xlsx", sheet = 3)

# Chloe's edit: gsub("(?<!HLA)-", "--", "TNFSF9-HLA-DPA1", perl = TRUE) to keep "HLA-DPA1" 
interactions.list$TLS.interactions <- gsub(pattern = "(?<!HLA)-", replacement = "--", interactions.list$TLS.interactions, perl = TRUE)
colnames(interactions.list)[1] <- "LR"
interactions.list.formerge <- interactions.list[,c(1,2,3,6)]

# Chloe's edit: merge source-target cell type pairs that correspond to the same LR into one row 
interactions.list.collapsed <- interactions.list.formerge %>%
  group_by(LR) %>%
  summarise(source = paste(source, collapse = "; "),
            target = paste(target, collapse = "; "),
            aggregate_rank = paste(aggregate_rank, collapse = "; "))

corrected_list.with.interaction <- lapply(corrected_df_list, function(x){
  left_join(x = x, y = interactions.list.collapsed, by = "LR")
}
)



11 Create a df with all data

slim.list <- list()

for(i in c(1:length(corrected_list.with.interaction))){
  df_i <- corrected_list.with.interaction[[i]] %>%
    select(LR, BH_dis15, BH_dis30, BH_dis100, BH_dis300, source, target, aggregate_rank) %>%
    mutate(Patient = names(corrected_list.with.interaction[i]))
  
  slim.list <- append(slim.list, list(df_i))
}

names(slim.list) <- names(corrected_list.with.interaction)
  
# Remove 108M
slim.list <- slim.list[names(slim.list) != "108M"]
merged_df <- do.call(rbind, slim.list)



12 Chloe’s edits to create a matrix

slim.list <- list()

for(i in c(1:length(corrected_list.with.interaction))){
  df_i <- corrected_list.with.interaction[[i]] %>%
    select(LR, BH_dis30, source, target, aggregate_rank) %>%
    mutate(Patient = names(corrected_list.with.interaction[i]))
  
  slim.list <- append(slim.list, list(df_i))
}

names(slim.list) <- names(corrected_list.with.interaction)
  
# Remove 108M
slim.list <- slim.list[names(slim.list) != "108M"]
merged30_df <- do.call(rbind, slim.list)



13 Take a subset of most interesting interactions for figure

subset_merged_df <- merged30_df %>%
  mutate(L = str_split_i(LR, "--", 1),
         R = str_split_i(LR, "--", 2)) %>%
  filter((LR %in% c("CXCL13--CXCR5", "GNAI2--CNR1", "CCL5--CXCR3", "CCL5--CCR5", "TNFSF9--HLA-DPA1", "SIRPG--CD47", "VCAM1--ITGA4_ITGB1", "CALM3--ESR1", "CD200R1--CD200", "IFNG--IFNGR1_IFNGR2", "CD70--TNFRSF13B", "ADAM28--ITGA4", "MDK--NCL", "LGALS3--LAG3", "CD48--CD2", "CD47--SIRPG", "CD38--PECAM1", "FAM3C--CLEC2D", "CD70--CD27", "FAM3C--PDCD1", "CD200--CD200R1", "FAM3C--LAMP1", "MDK--ITGA4_ITGB1", "TNFSF9--TNFRSF9", "CALR--ITGAV", "CCL5--CCRL2", "CCL5--CCR1", "ENTPD1--ADORA3", "CCL4--CCR1", "HSP90B1--TLR7", "B2M--LILRB1", "GNAI2--FPR1", "CSF1--CSF3R", "PMCH--MERTK", "LRPAP1--LRP1", "GNAI2--C5AR1", "CSF1--CSF2RA", "CSF1--SIRPA", "TIGIT--NECTIN2", "FADD--ABCA1", "LTA_LTB--LTBR", "PTPN6--CLEC12A", "FAM3C--LAMP1", "VCAN--ITGA4", "CCL4--CCR5", "TNFSF13B--HLA-DPB1", "CD86--CTLA4", "F13A1--ITGA4", "GNAI2--CXCR3", "ICAM1--ITGAL_ITGB2", "GNAI2--CCR5", "F13A1--ITGB1", "CXCL10--CXCR3", "HMGB1--HAVCR2", "GNAI2--F2R", "LGALS9--HAVCR2", "ICAM1--IL2RG", "ICAM1--SPN", "GRN--TNFRSF1B", "NECTIN2--TIGIT", "HBEGF--CD82", "LGALS3BP--ITGB1", "ADAM17--ITGB1", "CCL3--CCR5", "HGF--ITGB1", "SPP1--ITGA4_ITGB1", "SECTM1--CD7", "CD58--CD2", "SNCA--LAG3", "CALM3--EGFR", "CALM3--INSR", "CALR--ITGA3", "CALM3--AR", "ARF1--INSR", "ARPC5--LRP2", "GZMA--PARD3", "PTPN6--EGFR", "CCL5--DPP4", "APP--LRP10", "AGRN--ITGB1", "CCL2--CCR5", "MIF--CD74_CXCR4", "LGALS9-HAVCR2", "CD86-CTLA4", "CXCL13-CXCR5", "LTA_LTB-LTBR", "CD40LG-CD40", "FASLG-FAS", "CCL5-CCR5", "TNFSF14-TNFRSF14")) |
           # any interaction with a member starting with "CC", "CX", or "IL"
         str_detect(L, "^(CC|CX|IL)") |
           str_detect(R, "^(CC|CX|IL)")) %>%
  select(-c(L,R), -aggregate_rank)

subset_merged_df <- merged_df %>%
  mutate(L = str_split_i(LR, "--", 1),
         R = str_split_i(LR, "--", 2)) %>%
  filter((LR %in% c("CD86--CTLA4", "LGALS3--LAG3", "LGALS9--HAVCR2", "TNFSF9--HLA-DPA1", "CD47--SIRPG", "CXCL13--CXCR5", "SELPLG--SELL", "F13A1--ITGB1", "CD38--PECAM1", "CALR--ITGA3", "ICAM1--SPN", "ICAM1--ITGAL_ITGB2")) 
         # |
         #   # any interaction with a member starting with "CC", "CX", or "IL"
         # str_detect(L, "^(CC|CX|IL)") |
         #   str_detect(R, "^(CC|CX|IL)")
         ) %>%
  mutate(celltypes = case_when(LR %in% c("CD86--CTLA4", "LGALS3--LAG3", "LGALS9--HAVCR2") ~ "Immunosupp.\nmac-T",
                               LR %in% c("TNFSF9--HLA-DPA1", "CD47--SIRPG", "CXCL13--CXCR5", "SELPLG--SELL") ~ "B-T interactions",
                               LR %in% c("F13A1--ITGB1", "CD38--PECAM1", "CALR--ITGA3", "ICAM1--SPN", "ICAM1--ITGAL_ITGB2") ~ "Integrin/lymphocyte\ntrafficking")) %>%
  select(-c(L,R), -aggregate_rank)



14 Reformat for ComplexHeatmap

column_split <- subset_merged_df %>%
  distinct(LR, celltypes) %>%
  mutate(celltypes = factor(celltypes, levels = c("Immunosupp.\nmac-T", "B-T interactions", "Integrin/lymphocyte\ntrafficking")))

subset_merged_mat <- subset_merged_df %>% 
  select(LR, BH_dis30, Patient) %>%
  pivot_wider(values_from = "BH_dis30", names_from = "Patient") %>%
  column_to_rownames("LR") %>%
  as.matrix() %>%
  t()

subset_split <- subset_merged_df %>%
  select(LR, BH_dis30, Patient) %>%
  pivot_wider(values_from = "BH_dis30", names_from = "Patient") %>%
  column_to_rownames("LR") %>%
  t() %>%
  as.data.frame() %>%
  mutate(TLS = case_when(rownames(.) %in% c("107T", "102T", "108T") ~ "TLS+",
                         rownames(.) %in% c("109T", "110T", "111T") ~ "TLS-"),
         TLS = factor(TLS, levels = c("TLS+", "TLS-"))) %>%
  select(TLS)



15 Heatmap

col_fun = colorRamp2(c(0, 0.02, 0.11, 1), c("red", "red", "white", "blue"))

Heatmap(subset_merged_mat,
        cluster_rows = FALSE,
        row_split = subset_split$TLS,
        col = col_fun,
        column_split = column_split$celltypes,
        na_col = "grey70",
        cluster_columns = TRUE,
        cluster_column_slices = FALSE,
        heatmap_legend_param = list(
        title = "adj. P value of\n L-R colocalization", 
        at = c(0, 0.1, 1), 
        labels = c(0, 0.1, 1),
        direction = "horizontal"),
        border_gp = gpar(col = "black", lty = 1),
        show_row_dend = FALSE,
        show_column_dend = FALSE,
        row_names_side = "left",
        column_names_rot = 45)
crumbs: 3  Plot ligand and receptor spatial colocalization
